I want to finish building a team of voice agents using Livekit and Grok Voice Speech to Speech. I already have the xAI API, a paid Livekit Ship subscription, and Zoho One. From there, the agent figures out if the caller is a customer with a question or issue (battery pack not working, credit card charges, kiosk problems, etc.) or if they're a business wanting to partner. Customer service calls go to Zoho Desk under the Customer Service category. Sales/partner calls go to Zoho CRM (either update an existing contact or create a new lead).

It just needs some minor cleanup because the Maya agent is currently too open-ended instead of structured data collection. Victoria (customer service) should be more data-collect focused since people usually have specific questions, but she also needs to handle basic explanations (for example, explaining that a $35 charge is actually just a hold, not a final charge). This is important to avoid chargebacks.

At the end of every call, Livekit needs to send the full payload with transcript and summary to Zoho. I already have the agent sending data to Zoho once she confirms it, but I need to make sure that even if the caller hangs up or the call drops before confirmation, the data that was already collected still gets sent. Livekit has this feature built in.

I also want the same agents running as a chatbot on my website (currently Zoho SalesIQ). The chatbot should behave exactly the same as the voice agents so everything stays consistent. I'd like to do this inside Livekit if possible so it's easy to clone and deploy.

Once the inbound system is solid, we'll build an outbound sales agent. It will pull data from Zoho CRM (name, phone, email, company, and background info scraped ahead of time so the agent has context). After the call, it should update the contact record with notes and outcome, then trigger a Zoho flow to send a thank-you email with the call summary.

In total I need 3 agents: Inbound Customer Service (Victoria), Inbound Sales (Maya), and Outbound Sales. Plus the matching chatbot for the website and social channels.

I have two companies that will use this same tech stack with only minor differences in how the agents answer. One is the kiosk company, the other is a financial sales company. The data collected is basically the same, so once it's built for one, cloning it for the second should be easy. Please quote me for doing one company and for doing both.

The basics are already in place and partially tested. This is mostly about polishing, wiring everything together properly, and making sure the data flow is reliable even on dropped calls. A good developer should be able to get this done fairly quickly.

Please include in your reply:
- Your experience with Livekit Agents and Zoho One
- Fixed price quote for one company and for both
- Estimated timeline
- Any questions or suggestions

Ready to get started as soon as I find the right person.

• A dummy trading environment that streams live or near-live NSE options data, routes orders to Zerodha (or an equivalent broker’s API) in “paper” mode, and faithfully returns fills, greeks, and P&L as though we were trading real money. 
• Full support for automated algorithm executions in both forward and reverse modes—e.g., the same strategy should be able to flip direction or hedge automatically on command, with latency and slippage parameters configurable so we can stress-test ideas. 
• Seamless operation across 15 Windows workstations connected through a local switch; no cloud dependencies, everything must resolve locally over the LAN. 
• Clear instructions or scripts for deployment, plus a simple dashboard that lets the team start, stop, and monitor algos, review order logs, and download session reports.
* Net Postions per node / Total equivalent position management etc

PLEASE REIVEW THE .PDF FILE

If you have existing libraries in Python, Node, or Java that already speak to Zerodha’s Kite API, even better—just make sure they compile and run cleanly behind our firewall.

I’ll consider the project complete when we can fire off an options strategy from any node, see it executed in the sandbox with correct market feedback, and flip it into reverse mode on demand without manual intervention.
description_snippet: I’m building a sandbox-style proprietary options desk that runs entirely inside a 15-node LAN. The point is to mirror live NSE-options market behaviour as closely as possible so my team can develop and refine strategies without touching real capital.

I need a complete Shopify store built from the ground up to sell a line of physical products. The project starts with crafting a fully custom theme that reflects my brand’s personality and delivers a smooth shopping experience on desktop and mobile. 

Beyond the visual design, I also want the store configured with:

I will supply product photos, copy, and brand guidelines. You’ll handle theme design, layout, app selection or custom code (if needed) for the three features above, and launch-ready testing. Please outline your approach, timeline, and any past Shopify builds that show similar custom work.
